source_name: Wikipedia — List of books banned by governments (Singapore section)
_note: Curated from Wikipedia's Singapore section. Two cohorts: (A) Marxist/anti-colonial books banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order (1963-era), lifted in the 2015 unban of 240 publications; (B) recent Islamic/religious-content bans 2018-2021 under the Undesirable Publications Act 1967.

entries.title: Origin of Family, Private Property and State
entries.author: Friedrich Engels
entries.description: Banned in Singapore under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order, alongside other Marxist works. Lifted in the 2015 unban of 240 publications.

entries.title: Fanny Hill
title_variants: Memoirs of a Woman of Pleasure
entries.author: John Cleland
entries.description: John Cleland's 1748 erotic novel; banned in colonial-era Singapore (Crown Colony, post-1948). One of the 240 publications unbanned by the Singapore government in 2015.

entries.title: The Wisdom of Jihad
entries.author: Abuhuraira Abdurrahman
entries.description: Banned in Singapore under the Undesirable Publications Act (UPA) 1967 for promoting enmity among different religious communities.

entries.title: Things that Nullify One's Islaam
entries.author: Shaykh al-Islaam Muhammad ibn 'Abdil-Wahhaab
entries.description: Banned in Singapore under the Undesirable Publications Act (UPA) 1967 for promoting enmity among different religious communities.

entries.title: Red Lines: Political Cartoons and the Struggle Against Censorship
entries.author: Cherian George and Sonny Liew
entries.description: Banned in Singapore in 2021 under the Undesirable Publications Act (UPA) 1967 for reproducing materials offensive to religious feelings.

entries.title: Sex
entries._note_book_id: Matches existing book 12249 'Madonna Erotica' — the 1992 Madonna photo book. Adding SG 1992 ban to that book.
entries.author: Madonna
entries.description: Madonna's 1992 photo book (titled 'Sex'); banned in Singapore for explicit sexual content under the Undesirable Publications Act 1967.
